headlines.title: OpenAI, Meta, and SpaceXAI may be able to put pressure on Anthropic by emphasizing cost efficiency, as business customers increasingly scrutinize AI spending (Bloomberg)
Microsoft’s reset, a new era for Seattle startups, and how AI is changing everything for founders
Sources: Cursor is building a general-purpose AI agent codenamed Sand, aimed at non-developers, that handles emails, texts, and documents to rival Claude Cowork (Grace Kay/The Information)
Anthropic says it is extending Claude Fable 5 access on all paid plans, as well as keeping Claude Code's weekly rate limits 50% higher, through July 19 (The Economic Times)
Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
Meta spent a year being punished for its AI spending. Then it told investors how it would get the money back.
NYC-based Vendelux, a live B2B event information platform for CMOs and marketing and sales teams, raised a $50M Series B led by Tribeca Venture Partners (Lucia Moses/Business Insider)
Migrating a production AI agent to GPT-5.6: 2.2x faster, 27% cheaper
Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
Etzioni on AI: Who disagrees with you about AI? Here’s what the research shows
Zhipu’s founder says frontier AI should stay open to everyone. His own government may disagree.
The AI race has quietly stopped being about who has the biggest model
A look at the growing anti-AI movement in the Bay Area, as the disappearance of Sam Kirchner, co-founder of a hard-line activist group, has the movement on edge (Zusha Elinson/Wall Street Journal)
LARP – Revenue infrastructure for serious founders
AI has triggered the biggest gas-plant building boom in history, and a quiet fight to stop it
Sources: Apple is already taping out M7 with major NPU upgrades, and plans M7 Ultra with 1.5TB RAM, and M8, for 2028; new Pencils are coming with a new iPad Pro (Mark Gurman/Bloomberg)
Amazon has cut 57,000 jobs, and the people who kept theirs are not fine either
AI bosses say demand is ‘almost unlimited’. The market is no longer taking their word for it.
Most Americans now say the public should own half of the big AI companies
The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
Users of AI coding tools are flooding open-source projects with low-quality contributions, overwhelming maintainers and potentially eroding community engagement (Sam Learner/Financial Times)
AirDNA: during the FIFA World Cup group stage, platforms such as Airbnb saw 52K+ new listings in US host cities, while hotel bookings fell short of expectations (Financial Times)
A look at Bending Spoons' hiring process: the company, which owns Vimeo, AOL, and Evernote, received 800,000 job applications last year and made only 286 hires (Ben Cohen/Wall Street Journal)
Oregon’s Attorney General withdraws effort to delay Paramount and Warner Bros. merger
